title: Resume
text:
Professional Summary
Machine learning engineer and Data Scientist specializing in computational linguistics and speech processing, with hands-on experience building ASR/TTS pipelines and multi-speaker modeling (diarization, VAD, SER).

title: Resume
text:
Work Experience
Graduate Research Assistant | Vancouver, BC
Speech, Language and Interactive Media Lab, The University of British Columbia | Aug 2025 – Present

Investigate how diffusion-based models improve text-to-speech by developing end-to-end experimentation workflows—from dataset curation and preprocessing to model training, inference, and performance reporting.

title: Resume
text:
Research Experience
Undergraduate Research | Taipei, TW
Speech and Machine Intelligence Laboratory, National Taiwan Normal University
Principal Investigator: Dr. Berlin Chen | Nov 2024 – June 2025

Led a research project on multi-speaker Speech Emotion Recognition, improving alignment accuracy & data quality.
Developed a pipeline integrating WhisperX and Pyannote outputs into a novel alignment method, combining aligned audio segments with Wav2vec 2.0 and RoBERTa via bimodal cross-attention fusion.
Achieved +10% accuracy improvement on the IEMOCAP dataset, demonstrating the benefits of using aligned, clean segments for SER in multi-speaker settings.

title: Resume
text:
Projects
Annotated Corpus Platform with Interactive Interface | Remote
Github | Dec 2026

Built an end-to-end annotated corpus system with a FastAPI-based interactive interface, supporting data annotation workflow and backend service for scalable data management.
Containerized the system using Docker to ensure reproducibility and simplify deployment in production-like environments.

Automated Pipeline for TTS Quality Assessment via Speaker Verification Analysis | Vancouver, BC
UBC | Dec 2025

Developed an automated evaluation pipeline for a zero-shot voice cloning model (F5-TTS), processing over 2,000 audio segments from the LibriTTS dataset. Integrated SpeechBrain’s ECAPA-TDNN to extract speaker embeddings and quantified voice cloning performance using Cosine Similarity.

Text-to-Speech (TTS) Model Training | Taipei, TW
NTNU | Dec 2024

Implemented a FastSpeech2-based Text-to-Speech model using SpeechBrain, training on the LJSpeech 1.1 dataset.
Utilized checkpoint-based training to resume experiments efficiently and maintain reproducibility across training iterations.

Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) Project | Taipei, TW
NTNU | Sep 2024

Built an End-to-End ASR system using ESPnet2 with Transformer and Conformer models, achieving a 27.8% word error rate (WER) on the AN4 dataset using the Transformer model and compare that to Conformer.
Conducted comprehensive data analysis and optimized model parameters, resulting in a 15% performance improvement of the Transformer model over Conformer, and analyzed the stability and effectiveness in feature extraction and speech recognition tasks in both models.
